name: Dispute Assessment & Strategy
description: Adversarial multi-agent analysis of a contentious matter — elements, evidence, defences, jurisdiction, and remedies. Produces a case-strength assessment with a recommended strategy and cost-benefit comparison of litigation, arbitration, and ADR options. Jurisdiction-neutral.
taskDescriptionTemplate:
Assess the following contentious matter and produce a dispute-strategy report.

Matter: {{description}}

Documents provided: {{document}}

The assessment should:
1. Identify each cause of action and defence available to both sides; break each into its required elements.
2. Assess the evidence for and against each element — distinguish facts in dispute from pure-law questions.
3. Evaluate jurisdiction, forum, and governing law (including any forum-selection or arbitration clauses).
4. Give a reasoned case-strength rating (STRONG / ARGUABLE / WEAK) for each cause of action, with the decisive factors.
5. Assess limitation/prescription exposure.
6. Analyse available remedies and their quantification.
7. Compare the cost-benefit of: (a) litigation in the identified forum, (b) arbitration if available, (c) mediation / negotiated settlement.
8. Recommend a preferred strategy with the key near-term steps.
